description: Cross-implementation conformance vectors for verifyEvidenceProvenance (motebit/evidence-provenance@1.0). Each case: run the law over `input.bytes_utf8` (UTF-8 encoded to bytes) + `input.provenance`, with a resolver that handles exactly `input.resolvable_recipes`, and assert the structured result deep-equals `expected`. The law: the named `span` is an exact substring of projection(bytes), where the bytes hash to `provenance.digest`; projection is an injected, app-owned seam (absent ⇒ raw bytes; present+resolvable ⇒ apply the recipe; present+unresolvable ⇒ fail closed). Re-verifies PRESENCE, never truth, with no oracle. The recipe `agency.html-text.v1` is the published, world-public, byte-deterministic HTML→text projection (github.com/agency-computer/html-text-spec @ 01b475be); a conforming impl reproduces its output byte-for-byte. See ./README.md and spec/evidence-provenance-v1.md.

cases.name: raw-byte-present
cases.description: Raw-byte path (projection absent): the span is an exact substring of the raw bytes a re-fetcher obtains, content-addressed by the digest. The honest floor — re-verifiable by construction, no shared code.

input.bytes_utf8:
Total revenue for the quarter was $81,615 million, up 122% year over year.
Data Center revenue was $75,246 million.

cases.name: raw-byte-digest-mismatch
cases.description: The digest does not address these bytes → fail closed BEFORE any span check. A re-verifier handed the wrong primary record (or a tampered digest) must not proceed to locate a span.

input.bytes_utf8:
Total revenue for the quarter was $81,615 million, up 122% year over year.
Data Center revenue was $75,246 million.

cases.name: raw-byte-span-absent
cases.description: Digest matches, but the cited span is NOT in the bytes → span_absent. The anti-fabrication case: a producer cannot place a figure into a content-addressed record it does not contain.

input.bytes_utf8:
Total revenue for the quarter was $81,615 million, up 122% year over year.
Data Center revenue was $75,246 million.

cases.name: recipe-html-present
cases.description: Recipe path: bytes are raw HTML, digest is over the RAW HTML (honesty invariant), projection names agency.html-text.v1. A resolver that owns the recipe applies it to the raw bytes → 'Revenue $ 81,615 Data Center $ 75,246'; the span is present. Cross-language byte-determinism of the recipe is the §7 make-or-break, proven here across TS + Python.

cases.name: recipe-projection-unresolved
cases.description: Same artifact as recipe-html-present, but the verifier owns NO resolver for the recipe → FAIL CLOSED. A re-verifier that cannot reproduce the projection must NOT silently pass (domain-blind law: motebit owns no projection catalog). Resolver totality: 'cannot resolve' is signaled by omitting the resolver, never by a throwing one.

cases.name: recipe-html-span-absent
cases.description: Recipe resolvable and applied, digest matches, but the cited figure is absent from the projected text → span_absent. Anti-fabrication on the recipe path: applying the real recipe still cannot conjure a span the record does not contain.
